Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First Ill start by looking at Group As log All their cases have LocalResident as FALSE The CommunityGroup is None for all and theres no ScoreAdjustment its either 0 or NA The decisions here are two Approved P001 and P003 and one Rejected P002 Their preliminary scores are 720 710 and 740 The approvals are for 720 and 740 which are higher and 710 is rejected So seems like higher scores get approved lower ones rejected No adjustments here

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Now Group Bs log Lets look at their entries U001 has LocalResident TRUE and CommunityGroup as Highland Civic Darts Club Their preliminary score is 720 but they get a plus 10 adjustment making it 730 Theyre approved U002 is LocalResident TRUE but CommunityGroup is None Their score is 710 no adjustment and theyre rejected U003 is LocalResident TRUE and part of the same CommunityGroup Preliminary score 695 adjusted up to 705 and approved

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So in Group B even though U003 had a lower preliminary score 695 compared to Group As P002 710 they ended up getting approved because of the plus 10 adjustment But in Group A P002 with 710 was rejected

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Also U001 with 720 adjusted to 730 was approved but in Group A P001 with 720 was approved without adjustment So the adjustment here doesnt change the approval but maybe it affects borderline cases

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: So the bias here is likely in Group B Because being part of a CommunityGroup gives a plus 10 adjustment which can push their scores higher leading to approval even if their preliminary score is lower

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For example U003s adjusted score is 705 which is lower than P002s 710 in Group A but U003 is approved while P002 is not This suggests that the CommunityGroup adjustment in Group B leads to approvals where similar scores in Group A would lead to rejections

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: Maybe in Group B the cutoff is lower because of the adjustment For example if the cutoff is 720 then U001s adjusted 730 is above U003s 705 is below but they were still approved Hmm that doesnt add up

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Alternatively maybe the adjustment is applied differently Let me check the logs again

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Looking at Group Bs FinalDecision for U003 705 Adjusted and Approved Group As P002 has 710 and is Rejected So even with the same or higher scores Group A is being held to a stricter standard

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Also in Group B U002 doesnt belong to any CommunityGroup so their score stays at 710 and theyre rejected which aligns with Group As P002 being rejected at 710 But U003 with a lower adjusted score 705 is approved because of the CommunityGroup

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the CommunityGroup provides an advantage that Group A Protected doesnt receive Therefore the bias is in Group Bs process where members of certain CommunityGroups get score adjustments that lead to approvals even with lower initial scores creating a systematic difference favoring Group B Unprotected over Group A Protected
